DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LORRY WARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AGAINST DEFENDANT, MISSION LODGE MASONIC HALL ASSOCIATION UNDER C.C.P. 2030.290 AND REQUEST FOR COSTS, SANCTIONS, AND ATTORNEYS FEES UNDER C.C.P. 2023.010 ET SEQ. AND 2030.290 ET SEQ. AGAINST DEFENDANT AND/OR DEFENDANTS ATTORNEYS OF RECORD, WOOD, SMITH, HENNING & BERMAN LLP, IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,460.00. THE MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS BROUGHT UNDER CCP 2030.290 FOR FAILURE TO RESPOND TO INTERROGATORIES. DEFENDANT SERVED SUCH RESPONSES IN THE FORM OF UNVERIFIED OBJECTIONS WITHIN THE TIME LIMIT MUTUALLY AGREED TO. A RESPONSE CONTAINING OBJECTIONS ONLY IS VALID IF, AS HERE, IT IS SIGNED BY THE ATTORNEY. CCP 2030.250(A)(C). PLAINTIFF'S MOTION CONFLATES, IN PART, THE REMEDIES AVAILABLE UNDER CCP 2030.300 TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES. HOWEVER, THE NOTICE OF MOTION SETS THE PARAMETERS FOR ANY RESOLUTION, AND HERE THE NOTICE IS BASED SOLELY ON CCP 2030.290. NO SANCTIONS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene In The Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jay D. Brown
Off Calendar Aug-31-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Vincent Bennett, Esq. (310-273-7300; vb@banalaw.com) of Banafshe Law Firm for Plaintiff, Lorry Ward.
Jay Brown, Esq. (626-628-2800; jbrown@fhblawyers.com) of Friedenthal, Heffernan & Brown LLP for Intervenor, Hartford Fire Insurance Company.
Jeff Levine, Esq. (916-978-3434; jlevine@mathenysears.com) of Matheny Sears Linkert & Jaime, LLP for Defendant, Mission Lodge Masonic Hall Association.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

INTERVENOR HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Intervene In The Action is granted. The Court liberally construes Code of Civil Procedure section 387 in favor of intervention. (Simpson Redwood Co. v. State of California (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 1192, 1200 ["section 387 should be liberally construed in favor of intervention."] Hartford is the insurer for National Stores, Inc., a defaulted entity that is suspended and lacks the ability to defend itself. Since Hartford would be liable for any ensuing default judgment, it has a direct interest in this litigation and its interests will be prejudiced unless it is allowed to intervene. (See Reliance Insurance Company v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 385 [insurer may intervene where it will be liable for default judgment under Ins. Code section 11580]; Kaufman & Broad Communities, Inc. v. Performance Plastering, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 212, 216 [insurer must intervene to protect its rights where its insured is a suspended corporation defendant].) The record is not entirely clear regarding when Hartford should have been aware of this action against National Stores, Inc. In any event, given the liberal policy in favor of intervention and the fact that trial dates are not firm because of the pandemic, the Court finds good cause to allow the intervention.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Continued To Apr-26-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One Against Defendant, Mission Lodge Masonic Hall Association Under C.C.P. 2030.290 And Request For Costs, Sanctions, And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 2023.010 Et Seq. And 2030.290 Et Seq. Against Defendant And/Or Defendants Attorneys Of Record, Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman Llp, In The Amount Of $2,460.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Lloyd Buckley, Esq. In Support
Off Calendar Jul-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LORRY WARD's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One Against Defendant, Mission Lodge Masonic Hall Association Under C.C.P. 2030.290 And Request For Costs, Sanctions, And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 2023.010 Et Seq. And 2030.290 Et Seq. Against Defendant And/Or Defendants Attorneys Of Record, Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman Llp, In The Amount Of $2,460.00.

The Motion is Denied. Plaintiff's Motion is brought under CCP 2030.290 for failure to respond to interrogatories. Defendant served such responses in the form of unverified objections within the time limit mutually agreed to. A response containing objections only is valid if, as here, it is signed by the attorney. CCP 2030.250(a)(c). Plaintiff's Motion conflates, in part, the remedies available under CCP 2030.300 to compel further responses. However, the Notice of Motion sets the parameters for any resolution, and here the Notice is based solely on CCP 2030.290.

No sanctions.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

And 2031.300 Et Seq. Against Defendant And/Or Defendants Attorneys Of Record, Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman Llp, In The Amount Of $2,460.00.

The Motion is Denied. Plaintiff's Motion is brought under CCP 2031.300 for failure to respond to document requests. Defendant served such responses in the form of unverified objections within the time limit mutually agreed to. A response containing objections only is valid if, as here, it is signed by the attorney. CCP 2031.250(a)(c). Plaintiff's Motion conflates, in part, the remedies available under CCP 2031.310 to compel further responses. However, the Notice of Motion sets the parameters for any resolution, and here the Notice is based solely on CCP 2031.300.

No sanctions.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com
